component_identification.content: Pandemic base components in rulebook structure: 1 world board, 7 role cards, 7 matching pawns, 6 research stations, 96 disease cubes (24 per color across 4 diseases), 48 infection cards, 59 player cards (city cards, event cards, and epidemic cards), 4 cure markers, 1 infection-rate marker, 1 outbreaks marker, and rules/reference materials. Setup additionally uses infection discard pile, player discard pile, and deck partitions based on chosen difficulty (number of epidemic cards). Accurate cube and station counts are critical for legal loss checks and setup balance.

core_game_loop.content: Every player turn follows fixed sequence: perform up to 4 actions, draw 2 player cards, then infect cities according to current infection rate. Actions include movement, treating disease, sharing knowledge, building research stations, and discovering cures. Team play is fully cooperative, so table discussion and role coordination are expected. After each turn, pressure increases through infections and occasional epidemics, pushing the board toward outbreaks and resource depletion. The team wins instantly when all 4 cures are discovered before any loss condition triggers.

detailed_rules.content: Movement actions are Drive/Ferry to adjacent city, Direct Flight by discarding destination city card, Charter Flight by discarding your current city card, and Shuttle Flight between research stations. Treat Disease removes 1 cube in a city; if that disease is cured, a treat action removes all cubes of that color in the city. Discover Cure requires being at a research station and discarding a full color set (normally 5 cards, modified by role abilities such as Scientist). Epidemic cards resolve in three steps: Increase infection rate, Infect bottom-city with 3 cubes, then Intensify by shuffling infection discard pile and placing it on top of infection deck. Outbreak occurs when a cube would be added to a city already holding 3 of that color; instead, outbreak marker advances and each connected city gets one cube of that color. During one chain resolution, a city can outbreak at most once, preventing infinite loops while still allowing multi-city cascades. If cubes of a color run out when needed, the game is lost immediately. Event cards can be played outside normal turn timing as specified. Roles override baseline in targeted ways and are the primary source of tactical edge cases; resolve card text as written. Also note eradication timing: once a cured disease has no cubes on board, further infections of that color place no cubes. Teaching clarifications from rulebook-first play: always resolve effects in printed timing windows and finish mandatory steps before optional effects. If a card or tile text conflicts with a baseline rule, the specific card text takes priority for that interaction only. During teaching, call out hidden information boundaries, legal action checks before payment, and tie handling exactly as written. Most play disputes come from skipped sequence steps, so keep turn order visible and confirm each phase aloud in early rounds.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Pandemic has no point scoring; it is a binary cooperative result. Win condition: all four cures discovered immediately ends game in victory, regardless of board state. Loss conditions: outbreaks marker reaches 8, any disease cube supply cannot provide required cubes, or player deck is exhausted when cards must be drawn. Teaching emphasis: many games are lost by tempo collapse from epidemic intensify, not by a single bad turn, so preserving action economy is central.

beginner_strategy.content: Stabilize first, specialize second. Early turns should prevent outbreak chains in high-connectivity cities rather than chasing a premature cure at any cost. Build a compact research-station network to reduce travel actions; a few strong hubs outperform scattered stations. Route cards intentionally to one or two likely curers instead of splitting colors among everyone. Treating cured diseases is action-efficient board control and buys time for final cures. Save event cards for swing moments (outbreak prevention, critical mobility, epidemic aftermath), not minor convenience. Keep an eye on infection discard composition: recently hit cities are statistically likely to be hit again after intensify. New-player coaching tip: choose one clear plan by the end of early game, then spend midgame turns increasing action efficiency rather than collecting disconnected benefits. Track remaining rounds/end triggers constantly; many losses come from mis-timing rather than bad tactical choices. When uncertain between two similar options, prefer the move that preserves flexibility for the next turn while still scoring now.

metadata.notes: Rewrite sprint using official rulebook text as primary reference.
